questions.text: What would be the best alternative title for this passage?
options: A. How VR Brings Us All Together
B. The Future of VR Gaming
C. VR: A Useful Tool for the 21st Century
D. Saving the Planet with VR
questions.answer: C. VR: A Useful Tool for the 21st Century

questions.text: Which of these is NOT an example of embodied cognition?
options: A. watching a soccer game on TV
B. cooking a meal using a recipe
C. completing a crossword puzzle
D. playing a musical instrument
questions.answer: A. watching a soccer game on TV

questions.text: What is the purpose of the Ischia VR model?
options: A. to propose a new way of saving sea life near Ischia
B. to recreate what the climate in Italy was like in the past
C. to show how oceans are being damaged by climate change
D. to explain how pollution from Ischia is affecting other areas
questions.answer: C. to show how oceans are being damaged by climate change

questions.text: Which of the following is closest in meaning to adopters in paragraph D?
options: A. users
B. inventors
C. athletes
D. quarterbacks

questions.text: What is Bailenson's main point in the final paragraph (K)?
options: A. Although VR technology is improving, other media will still be important.
B. VR is a major change, but it will not fundamentally change who we are.
C. It will take several years before people's lives are really affected by VR.
D. VR's impact is significant, but many people in the world will not notice it.
questions.answer: B. VR is a major change, but it will not fundamentally change who we are.

questions.text: According to Bailenson, how do people learn best?
In what other way did VR help American football players, in addition to improving decision-making?
In what other way can VR avatars improve video conferencing, in addition to body posture and space?
What does the Ischia VR model allow people to be?
How is VR similar to other types of media?
Advantages of using VR to help athletes
matchingOptions: A. have clearer interactions with others
B. react to situations more quickly
C. visualize the impact on an environment over time
D. see a place they'd normally not be able to visit
E. avoid dangerous or life-threatening situations
F. practice the same action many times
answer: B. react to situations more quickly
F. practice the same action many times

questions.text: Advantages of using VR to improve video conferencing
matchingOptions: A. have clearer interactions with others
B. react to situations more quickly
C. visualize the impact on an environment over time
D. see a place they'd normally not be able to visit
E. avoid dangerous or life-threatening situations
F. practice the same action many times
questions.answer: A. have clearer interactions with others

questions.text: Advantages of using VR at the Ischia marine site
matchingOptions: A. have clearer interactions with others
B. react to situations more quickly
C. visualize the impact on an environment over time
D. see a place they'd normally not be able to visit
E. avoid dangerous or life-threatening situations
F. practice the same action many times
answer: C. visualize the impact on an environment over time
D. see a place they'd normally not be able to visit

questions.text: What does the interviewer suggest is one possible downside to VR?
Virtual reality therapy (VRT) is a way to ______ patients who suffer from psychological trauma.
Instead of lying down and just talking to a therapist, VRT patients take a less ______ role.
In this way, patients ______ experience a virtual version that is similar to the traumatic event.
Patients experience a virtual version that is similar to the ______ traumatic event.
By ______ the event virtually, patients are able to reduce their anxiety.
The downside of something is the ______ aspect of it.
options: A. positive
B. negative

questions.text: At a conference, you are more likely to speak to ______.
options: A. colleagues
B. family members

questions.text: If you demonstrate a skill, you ______ it.
options: A. show
B. hide

questions.text: You use conversely to indicate an idea is ______ another idea.
options: A. similar to
B. different from

questions.text: Places that don't get much rain can get flooded by a sudden ______.
For some people, a ______ of living in the suburbs is the long drive to work.
An increase in interest rates can cause a ______ in the housing market.
Once their children are grown up, some parents ______ to a smaller house.
